Article.ArticleTitle: Functional contribution and optimization of mix proportion of coal gangue, slag, and fly ash in the preparation of alkali-activated backfill materials.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study aimed to achieve the large-scale utilization of bulk solid waste by developing an alkali-activated cementitious material for backfill. The material was prepared from coal gangue (CG), ground granulated blast furnace slag (GGBS), and fly ash (FA), and activated by a composite alkaline activator consisting of water glass and calcium carbide residue. The influence of raw material ratio on the mechanical properties, fluidity, setting time and water separation rate of the backfill material was systematically explored, and the influence weight of each raw material on the backfill performance of the material was clarified. Furthermore, the main hydration products and the evolution of the microstructure of the cementitious materials under different ratios were analyzed. A carbon footprint calculation and comparative analysis were performed between the optimized cementitious material and traditional cement-based backfill materials. The results showed that the effect of CG and FA on mechanical properties was significantly affected by the GGBS content, and the key threshold was 50%. The relationship between the raw material ratio and the strength shows an opposite trend on either side of the threshold. When the ratio of CG to GGBS to FA was 4: 5: 1, the C40S50F10 cementitious material exhibited optimal comprehensive performance. Its compressive strength reached 13.7 MPa at 28 d, with excellent rheological properties and water retention capacity, meeting all the requirements for the backfill process. This study provides a theoretical basis and ratio reference for the development of high-performance solid waste-based backfill materials.
